description: Implement Theory Leverage Core: data model extension (LocalTheoryObject fields), theory prediction/update functions (B4), theory-contradiction interaction (B5), theory-guided verification (B1), theory-guided concept activation (B2), theory-guided routing (B3), and pipeline integration - all gated behind use_theory_leverage: bool = False
steps: Step 1 - Extend LocalTheoryObject in virtual_genesis/core/objects/theory.py: add fields predictive_value: float = 0.5, prediction_count: int = 0, correct_predictions: int = 0, explanatory_power: float = 0.0. Keep the create() classmethod and all existing fields unchanged.

Step 2 - Add B4 functions to virtual_genesis/runtime/theory_runtime/apply.py: (a) get_theory_prediction_for_task(theory: LocalTheoryObject, task_family: str, task_text: str) -> dict with keys 'predicts_difficulty' (bool), 'predicts_failure' (bool), 'confidence' (float), 'relevant_claims' (list[str]). Logic: check predictive_claims against task_text tokens, return predictions. (b) update_theory_predictive_value(theory: LocalTheoryObject, prediction_correct: bool) -> None. Logic: increment prediction_count, if correct increment correct_predictions, compute predictive_value = (correct_predictions + 1) / (prediction_count + 2) (Laplace smoothing). Include Arabic theft docstrings citing Popper (falsifiability) + Bayesian updating.

Step 3 - Add B5 function to virtual_genesis/runtime/theory_runtime/apply.py: check_theory_explains_contradiction(theory: LocalTheoryObject, contradiction: dict) -> bool. Logic: if contradiction's task_family is in theory.scope.task_families AND any of theory.predictive_claims tokens overlap with contradiction content tokens (threshold 2+ overlap), return True and update theory.explanatory_power += 0.1 (capped at 1.0). Include Arabic theft docstring citing Lakatos + Kuhn.

Step 4 - Add B1 function to virtual_genesis/runtime/verification_runtime/service.py: verify_output_theory_guided(task_family: str, output_text: str, theory_prediction: dict, framing_candidates=None, task_contract=None) -> dict. Logic: call base verify_output(), then if theory_prediction['predicts_failure'] is True, apply stricter checks similar to anomaly_aware (require 2 primary markers, require ALL properties). If theory_prediction['predicts_difficulty'] is True, require at least 1 secondary marker hit additionally. Include Arabic theft docstring citing Predictive Processing + Popper.

Step 5 - Add B2 function to virtual_genesis/runtime/concept_engine/apply.py: select_applicable_concepts_theory_guided(task_family: str, task_text: str, concepts: Iterable[ConceptCard], theory: LocalTheoryObject, limit=None, min_overlap=None, min_activation_score=None, task_contract=None) -> tuple[List[ConceptCard], List[ConceptActivationDecision]]. Logic: call base select_applicable_concepts(), then for each selected concept, if concept.id is in theory.concept_refs, boost its activation_score by +3 (or mark with notes='theory_boost'). Also if there are non-selected concepts whose id is in theory.concept_refs and their score is within 2 of fam_min_score, include them as well (theory-guided admission). Include Arabic theft docstring citing Theory-Theory of concepts + Explanation-Based Learning.

Step 6 - Add B3 function to virtual_genesis/runtime/economy_control/router.py: choose_tier_theory_guided(task: TaskObject, blackboard: BlackboardObject, memory_pack: BlackboardMemoryPack, theory_prediction: dict) -> TierDecisionObject. Logic: call base choose_tier(), then if theory_prediction['predicts_difficulty'] is True and confidence >= 0.5, prevent tier_0 (upgrade to tier_1 minimum). If theory_prediction['predicts_failure'] is True and confidence >= 0.6, force tier_2. Include Arabic theft docstring citing Bayesian Epistemology.

Step 7 - Integrate into pipeline virtual_genesis/runtime/pipeline/minimal_run.py: add parameter use_theory_leverage: bool = False. When enabled: (a) After memory retrieval, call select_applicable_theories to get theories for this task_family. (b) For the first applicable theory, call get_theory_prediction_for_task. (c) Pass prediction to choose_tier_theory_guided instead of base choose_tier. (d) If use_concepts is also True, call select_applicable_concepts_theory_guided instead of select_applicable_concepts. (e) Use verify_output_theory_guided instead of base verify_output. (f) After verification, call update_theory_predictive_value with prediction correctness = (prediction matched actual good_enough outcome). (g) For any contradictions, call check_theory_explains_contradiction. (h) Add to return dict: 'use_theory_leverage': use_theory_leverage, 'theory_prediction': prediction or None, 'theory_predictive_value': theory.predictive_value or None. IMPORTANT: When use_theory_leverage is False, behavior MUST be exactly as before (no changes to existing code paths). When BOTH use_theory_leverage and use_anomaly_leverage are True, theory leverage applies first, then anomaly leverage layers on top.
acceptance_criteria: LocalTheoryObject has 4 new fields: predictive_value, prediction_count, correct_predictions, explanatory_power
get_theory_prediction_for_task returns dict with predicts_difficulty, predicts_failure, confidence, relevant_claims
update_theory_predictive_value correctly updates predictive_value using Laplace smoothing
check_theory_explains_contradiction returns True when theory claims overlap contradiction content
verify_output_theory_guided applies stricter checks when theory predicts failure
select_applicable_concepts_theory_guided boosts scores of theory-aligned concepts
choose_tier_theory_guided prevents tier_0 when theory predicts difficulty, forces tier_2 when predicts failure
Pipeline with use_theory_leverage=False produces IDENTICAL results to current behavior
Pipeline with use_theory_leverage=True runs without errors
All 6 new functions have Arabic theft docstrings using the standard template
verification: Run: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q - all 88 existing tests pass

Run: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -c "from virtual_genesis.runtime.theory_runtime.apply import get_theory_prediction_for_task, update_theory_predictive_value, check_theory_explains_contradiction; print('OK')"

Run: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -c "from virtual_genesis.runtime.pipeline.minimal_run import run_minimal_pipeline; r = run_minimal_pipeline('test task'); assert r['use_anomaly_leverage'] is False; print('default unchanged')"

Run: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -c "from virtual_genesis.runtime.pipeline.minimal_run import run_minimal_pipeline; r = run_minimal_pipeline('test task', use_theory_leverage=True); print('theory leverage OK:', r.get('use_theory_leverage'));"
findings: All 88 existing tests pass. All 6 new functions verified via import and manual functional tests. Pipeline with use_theory_leverage=False produces identical output (theory_prediction and theory_predictive_value are None). Pipeline with use_theory_leverage=True correctly computes predictions when theories exist. Fixed a bug where the anomaly_aware tier routing lost its decision_reason update for the 0.4 severity threshold case during the str_replace operation. The concept_engine theory_guided function required importing LocalTheoryObject type annotation conditionally to avoid circular imports.
